turns.content: Jasmine on chassis is asking - "sanity check me: motor at 5000 rpm and 0.3 N*m through a 10:1 - I keep wanting to multiply both. right?"
Numbers go through calc here - no - speed divides, torque multiplies; here's both:

turns.name: calc
turns.content: = 500 rpm
And the torque, the other direction:

turns.name: calc
turns.content: = 3 m * N

Not both - and that instinct is exactly the trap. **Speed DIVIDES: 500 rpm out. Torque MULTIPLIES: 3 N*m out** (at 100% - you gave no efficiency, so that's the ideal ceiling; real will be a bit under). A gearbox can't add energy; it trades speed for torque, never boosts both.
